world.title: RFAs: Ending Impunity for International Crimes in Ukraine
world.description: Deadline: 08-May-2026 The International Renaissance Foundation grant competition supports civil society organizations working to investigate and prosecute international crimes in Ukraine. It funds projects that strengthen justice systems, support victims, and improve evidence collection for war crimes and human rights violations. Grants range from 1,000,000 to 4,000,000 UAH for projects lasting up to 12 months.

world.title: Apply now for SPAR Community Cashback Grant (United Kingdom)
world.description: Deadline: 13-May-2026 The SPAR UK Community Cashback Scheme 2026 provides £100,000 in total funding to support voluntary and community organisations across selected UK regions. Grants are distributed regionally through partners like SPAR UK Limited and its affiliates to fund grassroots initiatives that benefit local communities. world.title: Chakwera makes belated trip to mediate in election crisis
world.description: Commonwealth peace bid unlikely to rescue reputation of Hassan over rights abuses, Five months after opposition to the election of Tanzania’s Samia Suluhu Hassan with 98% of the vote as President on 29 October escalated into deadly street clashes, Malawi’s former president Lazarus Chakwera has arrived in Dar es Salaam in a belated effort to mediate. The stand-off between the presidency and the opposition continues and rights groups accuse Tanzania’s security forces and shadowy militia forces of killing hundreds of civilians.

business.title: Kenyan court clears path for Diageo’s US$2.3bn EABL sale
business.description: A Kenyan High Court ruling has removed a key legal obstacle to Diageo’s planned sale of its controlling stake in East African Breweries to Asahi. The decision allows the drinks giant to proceed with a major disposal as part of its wider debt reduction strategy.
